project.name: screenpipe
project.description: AI memory for your screen - open source desktop app (Tauri + Next.js + Rust) for continuous screen/audio capture with local AI search

nodes.summary: Rust build configuration file defining target-specific rustflags for x86_64-linux-gnu, aarch64-apple-darwin, and x86_64-apple-darwin. Sets AWS_LC_SYS_PREBUILT_NASM and CMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM environment variables. Configures parallel compilation with 16 jobs.

Release skill documentation for screenpipe monorepo. Covers releasing desktop app, CLI, and MCP components. Documents version bumping, GitHub Actions workflows (release-app.yml, release-cli.yml, release-mcp.yml), draft vs publish behavior, and troubleshooting build failures.

Screenpipe health check skill documentation. Provides commands for checking process status, recording status, disk usage, API endpoints (/health, /search, /audio/list, /vision/list, /audio/metrics, /vision/metrics), audio pipeline diagnostics with VAD/transcription metrics, database health checks, and macOS permissions verification via TCC.db.

Screenpipe logs skill documentation. Describes log locations (~/.screenpipe/screenpipe.YYYY-MM-DD.log and screenpipe-app.YYYY-MM-DD.log), tracing format, common grep patterns for finding errors, audio/OCR/DB issues, and log analysis guidelines.

Dev container Dockerfile based on Ubuntu 22.04 (mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/base:ubuntu-22.04). Installs Rust via rustup, system dependencies (ffmpeg, tesseract-ocr, clang, cmake, libavformat-dev, libssl-dev, libsoup-3.0-0, libgtk-3-dev, libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev, etc.), Bun runtime, and Tauri Driver (cargo install tauri-driver). Exposes ports 3000 and 3030.

Dev container configuration for Screenpipe. Forwards ports 3000 (app) and 3030 (API server). Runs postCreateCommand script after container creation. Uses ubuntu-22.04 base image with Dockerfile build.

Linux integration test script for dev container. Sets up X11 virtual display (Xvfb) with Openbox window manager, runs dependency installation, builds CLI with cargo build --release, sets up audio via pulseaudio, runs screenpipe, and executes OCR and audio capture tests. Checks logs for crashes at the end.
Shell script that defines 'test-linux' alias pointing to linux_integration.sh and adds it to .bashrc and .zshrc shell configurations.
Welcome message text shown in dev container. Informs user that environment is set up, 'test-linux' alias is configured, and explains how to run Linux build tests.

Gitattributes file configuring language detection for .rs (Rust) and .ts (TypeScript) files, marking build outputs (dist/, target/, node_modules/) as vendored, docs (*.md, *.txt) as documentation, and large files (*.onnx, *.wav, *.gif, *.mp4, *.safetensors, *.zip, *.tar.gz) for LFS storage.

Bug report issue template with fields for bug description, OS and version info, and additional context. Points users to log locations: ~/.screenpipe on macOS/Linux and %APPDATA%\.screenpipe on Windows.
Documentation issue template for reporting docs problems or suggesting improvements.
Feature request issue template asking for feature description, use case justification, alternatives considered, and additional context.
Question issue template for asking questions about screenpipe with fields for the question and relevant context.
GitHub configuration for request-info bot. Sets reply comment asking for more details, defines default titles to check, labels issues needing more info, excludes louis030195 and m13v from checks.
Pull request template with description field, related issue reference, and test instructions. Mentions incentive program ($20-$200) for reviewers who take >30 mins to review.
Audio test WAV file for testing audio capture functionality. Binary file stored in LFS.

Log checking script that validates screenpipe CLI output. Checks for: panic messages (indicates crash), 'Server listening on 0.0.0.0:3030' (successful startup), 'No windows found' error, and 'tesseract not found' error.

E2E test library providing shared helpers for all test scripts. Exports platform detection (IS_WINDOWS, IS_MACOS), test framework (suite, test, summary, screenshot), bb CLI wrappers (bb, assertExists, find, click, type, press, shortcut, scrape, tree), HTTP helpers (fetchJson, waitForHealth, httpStatus), process helpers (isScreenpipeRunning, getSystemInfo), and selectors (sel) for cross-platform accessibility testing.

E2E test runner that executes all test suites in sequence. Supports --suite filter flag. Records system info (hostname, OS, CPU, memory), checks screenpipe is running, verifies health API responds, then runs suites: launch, api, window, settings, recording, permissions, onboarding, chat, tray, windows, capture-fidelity, lifecycle.

REST API test suite for screenpipe server. Tests /health, /search (ocr, audio, query, pagination), /pipes/list endpoints. Validates response structures, date range queries, concurrent requests (5), large limit (100), and invalid content type handling (should not return 500).

App launch and health test suite. Verifies screenpipe process running, health API responding, frame_status and audio_status are 'ok', tray icon and menu items on macOS (recording indicator, version, settings, quit, changelog, show, stop/start recording, feedback), Windows tray pane exists. Tests status_code validity, timestamp info, internal error leakage prevention (S5.8), and concurrent API calls (S9.4).

Capture fidelity test suite validating OCR, audio, and input event capture. Runs 4 phases: (1) TextEdit typing + clipboard, (2) Terminal app switch + commands, (3) Finder window management, (4) Rapid app switching stress test. After 90s settling period, asserts: pipeline healthy, frame drop rate <20%, frames >0, OCR markers found (alpha, beta, verify), text input events, app switches >=50% expected, clipboard events, per-app OCR results (TextEdit, Terminal), accessibility data, CPU <150%, memory <2GB.

Chat UI test suite for macOS and Windows. On macOS: activates screenpipe-app, uses shortcut to show app, verifies web area loads and text input is focusable. On Windows: verifies screenpipe process alive via UIA tree lookup.

Lifecycle and stability test suite covering: concurrent DB access (50 requests, mixed read/write), COM thread conflict on Windows, CPU on idle screen, orphaned ffmpeg process detection, force quit + DB recovery, port 3030 conflict detection, health stability after restart, settings persistence, sleep/wake simulation, disk space check, large DB query performance (<2s for 100 results), frame skip rate on idle, clean quit verification, slow DB insert warning detection, rapid OCR query stress test (30 queries), and update mechanism accessibility.

data.name: CLAUDE.md
data.description: Project instructions for AI agents working on screenpipe
data.content_summary: Contains development guidelines: use bun for JS/TS, cargo for Rust, key directories (apps/screenpipe-app-tauri, crates/screenpipe-server, crates/screenpipe-audio, crates/screenpipe-vision), testing procedures (cargo test, bun test), macOS dev build signing info, and git workflow guidelines

data.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
data.description: Contribution guidelines for screenpipe project
data.content_summary: Detailed guide covering installation on macOS/Windows/Linux, Docker setup, testing procedures, debugging tips (tokio-console, addr sanitizer), benchmark commands, database migration guidance, and Azure VM setup for remote development

data.name: VISION.md
data.description: Product vision and principles for screenpipe
data.content_summary: Defines screenpipe as context infrastructure for AI agents - ambient layer between humans and digital work. Core vision: Record, Rewind, Ask. Emphasizes stability over features, local-first data storage, cross-platform support, and open source by default. Design voice is black/white minimalist.

data.name: TESTING.md
data.description: Regression testing checklist for screenpipe development
data.content_summary: Comprehensive checklist covering 29 categories of testing including: window overlay/fullscreen spaces, dock/tray icons, monitor plug/unplug, audio device handling, frame comparison/OCR pipeline, battery saver mode, permissions, Apple Intelligence, app lifecycle, database/storage, AI presets, onboarding, timeline/search, sync/cloud, Pipes, Windows-specific tests, and CI/CD

data.name: DESIGN.md
data.description: Design guide for screenpipe UI/UX
data.content_summary: Black & white geometric minimalism design philosophy. Typography: Space Grotesk (headings), Crimson Text (body), IBM Plex Mono (code). Colors: grayscale only with light/dark modes. Sharp corners (0px radius), 1px solid borders, no shadows. 150ms transitions, uppercase buttons, lowercase titles. Key files: globals.css, tailwind.config.ts, colors.ts, ui components

data.name: README.md
data.description: Main README for screenpipe project
data.content_summary: AI memory for screen - captures screen and audio locally, stores in SQLite, provides AI-powered search. Features: event-driven capture, audio transcription (Whisper), AI search, timeline view, Pipes (plugin system), MCP server integration, Apple Intelligence support. Supports macOS/Windows/Linux. License: MIT. Built with Tauri + Next.js

data.name: .devcontainer/Dockerfile
data.description: Dev container configuration for VSCode remote development

nodes.name: ReadContent

nodes.name: ShortcutGate
nodes.description: Onboarding slide for global shortcut capture. Displays animated key caps for the configured shortcut (default Cmd+Ctrl+S) and listens for shortcut-show Tauri event to complete onboarding. Shows skip button after 30s.

nodes.name: OnboardingStatus
nodes.description: Onboarding slide showing permission checks and engine startup progress. Manages screen recording, microphone, and accessibility permissions on macOS. Auto-starts screenpipe engine when permissions granted.

nodes.name: PipeAIIcon / PipeAIIconLarge / PipeAIIconStatic
nodes.description: SVG pipe icon component with flowing data particles animation. Three variants: standard (24px), large (48px with decorative Escher-inspired corner accents), and static (no animation).

nodes.name: PipeInstallDialog
nodes.description: Alert dialog for installing pipes via deep link. Supports both registry: slugs and external URLs. Fetches registry details for permission review before installation. Dispatches pipeInstalled event after successful install.

nodes.name: PipeMonitorView + DeviceCard + PipeRow
nodes.description: Multi-device pipe monitoring view. Shows online/offline status for remote screenpipe instances, displays pipe execution history, allows starting/stopping pipes and toggling enable state. Includes SSH deploy dialog.

nodes.name: PipeStoreView + DiscoverView + PipeDetailPanel + PublishDialog + PermissionsReview
nodes.description: Full pipe store for discovering, installing, publishing, and reviewing community pipes. Features permission review, secret redaction before publishing, fork-to-chat integration, and stale-while-revalidate caching.

nodes.name: PostInstallConnectionsModal
nodes.description: Modal dialog shown after pipe installation to configure required connections. Fetches available integrations from /connections API and shows per-connection status (configured/not configured).

nodes.name: PrettyLink
nodes.description: Styled anchor link component with filled and outline variants. Uses Tailwind CSS for styling with focus ring accessibility.

nodes.name: AIPresetsSelector + AIProviderConfig + AIPresetDialog + PermissionsReview
nodes.description: AI provider/preset configuration component. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, Custom, ChatGPT, and Screenpipe Cloud providers. Includes model selection, API key management, OAuth flows, and preset CRUD operations.

nodes.name: ChatMessageActions
nodes.description: Copy-to-clipboard action button for chat messages. Shows check icon on success, uses useCopyToClipboard hook with 2s timeout.

nodes.name: ChatMessage
nodes.description: Chat message renderer with AI model icon detection, thinking/reasoning display, video embedding for .mp4 links, screenpipe:// deep links for timeline navigation, mermaid diagram rendering, and code syntax highlighting.

nodes.name: CurrentFrameTimeline
nodes.description: Main frame viewer for timeline. Handles video seeking, snapshot frames, OCR text overlay, accessibility text, URL detection, native macOS Live Text, context menu with frame actions (copy image/text/deeplink, ask about frame, run pipe).

nodes.name: DailySummaryCard
nodes.description: Apple Intelligence daily summary card. Computes app usage time breakdown from OCR frames, fetches audio transcripts, generates AI summary via /ai/chat/completions, auto-triggers at 6pm if plugged in, with localStorage caching and generation locking.
key_features: App usage computation from OCR frames

nodes.name: ExportButton
nodes.description: Video export button for timeline selection. Uses WebSocket to stream frame extraction and encoding, shows progress, supports Tauri save dialog or browser download fallback.

nodes.name: useDateNavigation
nodes.description: Hook for timeline date navigation. Handles date changes with nearest-date-with-frames lookup, direct navigation to search results, pending navigation resolution when frames arrive, and jump-to-today.

nodes.name: useFrameActions
nodes.description: Hook providing frame action handlers: copy image, copy frame text, copy deeplink, ask about frame, run pipe with frame context.

nodes.name: useFrameLoading
nodes.description: Frame loading hook with video seek, snapshot direct load, fallback image, FPS auto-calibration, chunk failure tracking with TTL, adjacent frame preloading, and ResizeObserver for dimensions.
key_features: Snapshot frame (JPEG/PNG) direct load via asset protocol

nodes.name: useLiveText
nodes.description: Hook for native macOS VisionKit Live Text overlay integration. Initializes ImageAnalysisOverlayView, manages position updates, search term highlights, and click guards for UI elements.

nodes.name: useScrollZoom
nodes.description: Hook for scroll-to-navigate and pinch-to-zoom in timeline. Handles wheel events, native magnify/scroll Tauri events, search result navigation, filter-aware frame matching, and RAF-based smooth zoom animation.
key_features: Scroll navigation with zoom-based speed scaling
Native scroll via Tauri event (embedded mode)

nodes.name: useSearchFocus
nodes.description: Focus management hook for search modal input. Handles mount focus, focus guard restoration, and periodic focus checks. Uses escalating retry delays for macOS window server timing issues.

nodes.name: useTimelineFilters
nodes.description: Hook for timeline frame filtering by device, app, domain, speaker, tag, and meeting. Computes matching frame indices, provides snap functions for each filter type.

nodes.name: useTimelineKeyboard
nodes.description: Keyboard navigation hook for timeline. Handles arrow keys for frame navigation, Alt+arrow for app boundaries, Cmd+Shift+C for copy frame, Escape handling, slash for search modal, and search result review mode.
key_features: Escape sequence (search review -> search modal -> filters -> close)
Search result review mode with arrow keys

nodes.name: ImageGrid + MainImage + FrameImage + SkeletonCard
nodes.description: Search results image grid and main image viewer with text overlay. Supports keyboard navigation (arrow keys), infinite scroll pagination, retry logic for failed images, and object-contain letterbox rendering.

nodes.name: MemoizedReactMarkdown
nodes.description: Memoized wrapper for react-markdown with shallow comparison on children and className props.
Renders Mermaid diagrams with Screenpipe brand theming (grayscale, sharp corners). Detects dark mode and applies appropriate theme variables.
OCR overlay for region selection. Shift+drag to select screen region, extracts text via screenpi.cloud API, copies to clipboard via Tauri.
Simple search input component with Search icon and auto-focus capability.

Large search modal with keyword search across OCR text/audio/UI events, speaker search (@), tag search (#), frame thumbnails, faceted filtering (apps, domains, time), infinite scroll, keyboard navigation.
Simple SVG loading spinner component.
Renders yellow highlight boxes over matching text in thumbnails based on search terms. Uses useFrameTextData hook with LRU cache.

Main timeline component displaying frames, audio playback, search integration, meeting detection, subtitle bar, timeline controls, daily summaries. Handles keyboard shortcuts, window focus events, deep linking.
AI agent implementations for timeline analysis: recursive-summarizer (chunks), context-master (all context), window-tracker (apps/windows), text-scanner (OCR), voice-analyzer (audio transcriptions).
AI chat panel for querying timeline data. Features agent selection, streaming responses via Pi events, draggable/resizable window, context building from frames, clipboard copy, PostHog tracking.
Context popover showing top windows, URLs, UI events (keystrokes/clicks/clipboard), audio transcripts for selected app. Fetches from local HTTP server, uses createPortal.
Audio transcript panel with conversation view, speaker assignment (chunk-level and bulk), retranscription, meeting detection, copy/summarize/send-to-chat actions.
Utility functions: extractDomain (strips www), getFaviconUrl (Google Favicon API), FaviconImg component with fallback to app-icon.
Displays detected meetings as pills on timeline. Shows app type (Zoom/Teams/Meet/etc), speaker count, duration. Hover tooltip with speaker list and audio preview.
Displays audio transcriptions as subtitles with active/lookahead entries, speaker labels, auto-scroll, collapse/expand. Shows transcription paused indicator when in meeting.
Timeline controls: date navigation, calendar picker, jump-to-today, play/pause with speed control, device mute dots, search button, chat button.
Shows app icons and audio markers on timeline. Fetches icons from localhost:11435/app-icon, caches in localforage. Dialog shows window history for selected app.
Mouse drag selection on timeline. Creates selection range from chunk indices.
Tag toolbar for timeline selection. Quick tags, custom tags, delete range, re-transcribe, pipe radial menu. Uses useTimelineSelection hook.

Main timeline slider displaying frames grouped by app with color coding. Features filters (device/app/domain/speaker/tag/meeting), zoom control, chat history overlay, day boundary dividers, app context popovers.

Icon components: NextChat, OpenAI, Vercel, GitHub, Arrow, User, Plus, Spinner, Message, Trash, Refresh, Stop, Sidebar, Moon, Sun, Copy, Check, Download, Close, Edit, Share, Users, ExternalLink, ChevronUpDown, Llama, Code, Ollama, Claude, Gemini.
Radix UI Popover wrapper components: Popover, PopoverTrigger, PopoverContent, PopoverContentInDialog.
Renders video/audio from local file path via getMediaFile action. Handles retry logic (3 attempts), audio/video detection, blob URL creation.
Screenpipe status display showing frame/audio recording status, server control (restart), permission buttons (screen/audio), data directory access.

Renders OCR text as invisible but selectable overlay over screenshots. Uses browser native selection engine. Groups blocks into lines with column gap splitting. Exports getSelectableLayerText and clearSelectableLayerSelection.
Account management UI: login/logout, Stripe checkout, subscription status (Pro features), pipe sync across devices, referral card, deep link handling for Stripe OAuth.
Referral program card component - displays user's referral code, link copying, and email invite functionality. Integrates with screenpi.pe API for referral data.
Data retention management with auto-delete old recordings. Provides configurable retention period (7-90 days), manual cleanup trigger, and status display. Polls /retention/status API.
Recording schedule configuration with visual timeline grid. Supports per-day time ranges with modes: all/audio_only/screen_only. Includes presets (weekdays 9-5, always on) and custom rules.
Individual keyboard shortcut configuration row with recording capability. Detects conflicts, syncs with Rust backend via updateGlobalShortcuts. Uses hotkeys-js for key capture.

Section container for keyboard shortcuts management. Renders ShortcutRow components for global shortcuts (showScreenpipe, showChat, search, recording start/stop, audio start/stop). Toggles shortcut reminder overlay.
Tabbed storage settings container. Tabs: Local (DiskUsageSection), Archive (ArchiveSettings), Sync (cloud sync - feature flagged by PostHog 'cloud-sync').

Comprehensive cloud sync settings with multi-step flow: onboarding (subscription check), password setup (for new sync), active sync management. Uses zero-knowledge encryption, supports multiple devices, reveals encryption key for device setup. Polls subscription status after checkout.

Team management interface with create/join team, member management, shared configs (pipes, window filters, URL filters). Supports deep link invites, passphrase-based secure invites, end-to-end encrypted config sharing. Role-based (admin/member) UI.

AI usage tracking dashboard showing model requests per provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, etc.). Tracks chat responses and pipe executions separately. Caches data in ~/.screenpipe/usage-cache.json. Time range filtering (24h, 7d, 30d, all).
Voice memos indexing card for macOS. Opens Full Disk Access system preferences. Displays instructions for enabling Voice Memos indexing in Privacy & Security.

Log sharing component for feedback/debugging. Captures last 5 minutes of screen recording, screenshot upload, optional chat history inclusion (PII redacted). Uploads to screenpi.pe and returns shareable link.

Invisible tracking component for PostHog analytics. Listens to Tauri shortcut events (show_screenpipe, show_chat, etc.) and tray menu events. Exports formatShortcut() for platform-aware display (macOS symbols).

Speaker assignment popover for audio transcription chunks. Searches speakers via API, supports creating new speakers. Two-phase: instant assignment + background propagation to similar voices. Includes audio preview and 'mark as hallucination/noise' option.
Speaker display badge with click-to-assign. Shows speaker name or 'Unknown #id'. Brand styling with sharp corners, hover invert effect.
Animated splash screen with screenpipe logo, pulsing glow effect, loading dots animation. Uses framer-motion for animations.

Main chat interface component (3876 lines). Features: AI conversations via Pi agent, screenpipe data search (OCR, audio, UI events), mention system (@today, @audio, @screen), tool call execution, markdown rendering, image attachments, conversation history, follow-up suggestions. Builds dynamic system prompt with search rules and API references.

Persistent permission warning banner for macOS. Shows missing screen recording, microphone, and accessibility permissions. Polls every 5 seconds and listens to 'permission-lost' events. Auto-requests permission on click.

Permission request buttons for screen and audio access. Shows permission status (check/x icons), requests permissions via Tauri commands, opens system settings. Polls microphone permission every second.

OCR text overlay for screenshots. Detects and makes URLs clickable, highlights search term matches, click-to-copy text blocks. Includes URL validation to filter OCR false positives (garbled text, bad TLDs). TextOverlayForImage wrapper for proper positioning.
React context for theme management. Supports system/light/dark modes. Persists to localStorage, syncs with Tauri native theme via set_native_theme. Listens to system preference changes.
Theme toggle button with icon (Moon/Sun). Exports ThemeToggle (icon only) and ThemeToggleWithLabel variants. Uses useTheme hook.
Shadcn/Radix UI accordion primitive. Exports Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent.

Shadcn/Radix UI alert dialog primitive. Exports AlertDialog, AlertDialogTrigger, AlertDialogPortal, AlertDialogOverlay, AlertDialogContent, AlertDialogHeader, AlertDialogFooter, AlertDialogTitle, AlertDialogDescription, AlertDialogAction, AlertDialogCancel.
Shadcn alert component with variants (default, destructive). Exports Alert, AlertTitle, AlertDescription with CVA variant styling.
Shadcn badge component with variants (default, secondary, destructive, outline). Uses CVA for styling.

nodes.summary: Main entry point for screenpipe Tauri desktop app (1776 lines). Sets up Tauri builder with all plugin initializations (store, notification, process, shell, fs, http, dialog, log, client, analytics), registers ~50 Tauri commands, spawns embedded server, health check service, analytics manager, calendar publisher, ICS poller, remote sync auto-start, and pipe suggestions scheduler auto-start. Includes panic hook with Sentry integration and startup sequencing with port cleanup.

FFI bridge to SwiftUI notification panel on macOS (60 lines). Exposes `notif_is_available`, `notif_show`, `notif_hide`, `notif_set_action_callback` via `extern "C"` blocks with no-op stubs on non-macOS. Handles JSON payload with id, type, title, body, actions, autoDismissMs fields.

FFI bridge to SwiftUI shortcut reminder panel on macOS (65 lines). Similar pattern to native_notification with `shortcut_show`, `shortcut_hide`, `shortcut_set_action_callback` via `extern "C"`. No-op stubs on non-macOS.

AnalyticsManager wrapping PostHog API client. Methods: `fetch_attribution` (UTM from website), `send_event`, `start_periodic_event` (health + feature config). Uses disk_usage cache for analytics payload. Skips analytics in debug mode. Configured via POSTHOG_API_KEY and POSTHOG_HOST environment variables.

Calendar integration for macOS (EventKit) and Windows (calendar_windows). `CalendarStatus` enum (Unknown, Authorized, Denied, Restricted), `CalendarEventItem` struct with id, title, startTime, endTime, isOnline, meetingUrl. Commands: `calendar_status`, `calendar_authorize`, `calendar_get_events`, `calendar_get_current_meeting`. Background publisher sends events every 60s via `screenpipe_events::send_event`. Falls back to ics_calendar.

PKCE OAuth flow for ChatGPT/OpenAI. CLIENT_ID = 'app_EMoamEEZ73f0CkXaXp7hrann'. Tokens stored in ~/.screenpipe/chatgpt-oauth.json. Commands: `chatgpt_oauth_login`, `chatgpt_oauth_status`, `chatgpt_oauth_get_token`, `chatgpt_oauth_models`, `chatgpt_oauth_logout`. Local TCP callback server on port 1455. Supports code_verifier, code_challenge, state param validation.

Large Tauri command module (1931 lines). Key commands: `show_main_window`, `hide_main_window`, `show_shortcut_reminder`, `show_notification_panel`, `hide_notification_panel`, `get_disk_usage`, `list_cache_files`, `delete_cache_files`, `open_login_window`, `open_google_calendar_auth_window`, `show_window`, `close_window`, `resize_search_window`, `search_navigate_to_timeline`, `copy_frame_to_clipboard`, `copy_deeplink_to_clipboard`, `copy_text_to_clipboard`, `perform_ocr_on_image`, `rollback_to_version`, `update_show_screenpipe_shortcut`, `register_window_shortcuts`, `unregister_window_shortcuts`, enterprise license key management, audio device listing, monitor listing.

Data directory resolution and validation. `resolve_data_dir` checks SCREENPIPE_DATA_DIR env var, then store settings, falls back to ~/.screenpipe. `validate_data_dir` Tauri command. `is_e2e_mode()` checks build feature flag.

Disk usage analysis (376 lines). `DiskUsage`, `MonitorUsage`, `DiskUsedByMedia`, `DiskUsedByOther`, `CachedDiskUsage` structs. `disk_usage` async function with 1-hour cache at ~/.cache/screenpipe/disk_usage.json. Scans media files via regex to extract monitor names. Finds oldest recording date. Uses `sysinfo` crate for disk space. Parses filenames like `2024-01-01_00-00-00__monitor_1__audio.mkv`.

macOS dock right-click menu using objc runtime (160 lines). Registers `ScreenpipeDockMenuDelegate` class with `showScreenpipe:`, `openSettings:`, `checkUpdates:`, and `applicationDockMenu:` methods. Uses method swizzling to add methods to existing app delegate. Provides quick access to app, settings, and update checking from dock icon.

Embedded server startup (624 lines). `start_embedded_server` async function builds full screenpipe recording stack: database (SQLite via screenpipe_db), audio manager, vision manager, UI recorder, meeting detector, pipe manager, hot frame cache, power manager, sleep monitor, schedule monitor, snapshot compaction. Creates `EmbeddedServerHandle` for shutdown control. Binds HTTP listener before returning.

Hardware capability detection using `sysinfo` for CPU/memory info. Checks `metal`/`vulkan` features. Always recommends 'parakeet' ONNX model (no GPU required). Provides `detect_hardware_capability` function.

Health check service (1206 lines). `start_health_check` polls localhost:3030/health every 1s. `RecordingStatus` enum (Starting/Recording/Stopped/Error) with `decide_status` logic. Features: debounced status transitions (30 consecutive failures threshold), DRM auto-pause/resume, capture stall detection (90s threshold), wake-from-sleep reset, permission monitoring integration. Updates tray icon based on status + theme (light/dark).

Cross-platform app icon retrieval. macOS uses NSWorkspace + NSBitmapImageRep for PNG extraction. Windows uses registry search (App Paths, Uninstall), PowerShell Get-AppxPackage, and direct path heuristics. Linux uses freedesktop .desktop files + GTK icon theme + SVG→PNG conversion via resvg.

ICS/webcal feed parsing (473 lines). Extensive Windows timezone→IANA mapping (80+ timezones). `parse_ics_to_events` extracts VEVENT data. Poller fetches feeds every 10 minutes, deduplicates by UID, sends on event bus. Commands: `ics_calendar_get_entries`, `ics_calendar_save_entries`, `ics_calendar_test_url`, `ics_calendar_get_upcoming`. Windows tz names like 'Central Standard Time' mapped to IANA 'America/Chicago'.

macOS Live Text (VisionKit) FFI wrapper (361 lines). Single worker thread (ANALYZE_WORKER OnceLock with sync_channel capacity 1) prevents thread pile-up. Generation counter (ANALYZE_GENERATION AtomicU64) skips stale requests. Commands: `livetext_is_available`, `livetext_init`, `livetext_analyze`, `livetext_prefetch`, `livetext_update_position`, `livetext_highlight`, `livetext_clear_highlights`, `livetext_hide`, `livetext_set_guard_rect`.

FFI declarations for Live Text (42 lines): `lt_is_available`, `lt_init`, `lt_analyze_image`, `lt_prefetch`, `lt_update_position`, `lt_highlight_ranges`, `lt_clear_highlights`, `lt_hide`, `lt_destroy`, `lt_set_guard_rect`, `lt_remove_guard`, `lt_free_string`. Swift FFI bridge to VisionKit/NSSpellChecker.
Log file management. `get_log_files` Tauri command listing .log files from data dir and screenpipe dir. `get_data_dir` resolves from store settings. Returns file paths with metadata.

Comprehensive macOS TCC permission management (1038 lines). `do_permissions_check`, `request_permission`, `reset_and_request_permission`, `check_microphone_permission`, `check_screen_recording_permission`, `check_accessibility_permission_cmd`. Browser automation for Chromium browsers (Arc, Chrome, Brave, Edge, Vivaldi, Opera, Chromium) with `ae_check_automation_direct` FFI using AEDeterminePermissionToAutomateTarget. `start_permission_monitor` background task polls every 10s with consecutive failure tracking (2 required) and 5-minute emit cooldown. Defines CHROMIUM_BROWSERS const.

Pi coding agent process management (2578 lines). `PiPool` holds multiple `PiManager` sessions. RPC protocol over stdin/stdout. `PiCommandQueue` serializes commands. `spawn_queue` creates mpsc channel with `PiQueueHandle` and `PiQueueState`. Commands: `pi_start`, `pi_stop`, `pi_prompt`, `pi_abort`, `pi_new_session`, `pi_info`, `pi_check`, `pi_install`, `pi_update_config`. Provider config supports: screenpipe-cloud, openai, openai-chatgpt, native-ollama, anthropic, custom. Installs pi via bun to ~/.screenpipe/pi-agent/. Background installation via `ensure_pi_installed_background`.

Command queue for Pi SDK serialization (451 lines). `WaitMode::StreamThenWaitDone` (replies immediately, waits for done) vs `WaitMode::WaitDone` (blocks until done). `PiQueueState` with `Notify` for done/terminated signals. `wait_for_done_or_terminated` with 300s timeout. Abort drains and cancels all pending commands. Includes unit tests for queue state, handle send, and abort cancellation.

Periodic notifications for automation ideas (194 lines). `PipeSuggestionsState` manages tokio join handle. `start_scheduler_inner` calculates initial delay based on last_shown_at and frequency (5 min startup delay or remaining time). Fires notification via `show_pipe_suggestion` → `commands::show_notification_panel`. `auto_start_scheduler` restores scheduler from saved settings on app launch. Commands: `pipe_suggestions_get_settings`, `pipe_suggestions_update_settings`.

Recording state management (579 lines). `RecordingState` with embedded server handle and spawn epoch tracking. `spawn_screenpipe` with cooldown enforcement (30s between restarts), port cleanup, permissions check, `kill_process_on_port` for orphaned processes (lsof on unix, netstat+taskkill on Windows). `get_audio_devices` and `get_monitors` commands.

Remote sync Tauri commands (78 lines). Wraps screenpipe_connect::remote_sync and screenpipe_connect::sync_scheduler. Commands: `remote_sync_test`, `remote_sync_now`, `remote_sync_discover_hosts`, `remote_sync_start_scheduler`, `remote_sync_stop_scheduler`, `remote_sync_scheduler_status`. Uses SSH hosts discovery and SyncScheduler for background sync.

Safe tray icon wrappers (55 lines). `is_valid_icon` checks width>0 && height>0. `safe_set_icon` and `safe_set_icon_as_template` wrappers that skip invalid icons to prevent muda 0.17.1 panic with ZeroWidth. Logs warnings for skipped invalid dimensions.

nodes.description: NSPanel visibility management for macOS overlay/window mode. Handles show_panel_visible sequence (save_frontmost_app -> alpha:1 -> order_front -> make_key -> first_responder -> emit). Manages MAIN_PANEL_SHOWN atomic flag and MAIN_CREATED_MODE tracking.

Core window creation and lifecycle management. Defines RewindWindowId enum (Main, Home, Search, Onboarding, Chat, PermissionRecovery) and ShowRewindWindow. Handles window mode vs overlay mode, focus management, auto-hide on focus loss with 300ms debounce, scale factor change handling, and multi-platform window creation (macOS NSPanel, Windows overlay, Linux).

Utility functions for window management: with_autorelease_pool (macOS autorelease pool management), run_on_main_thread_safe (panic-safe main thread execution), screen_aware_size (clamp window to 85% of monitor).
Window module entry point. Re-exports all submodules and provides finalize_webview_window as single post-creation entrypoint. Registers content_process_handler during finalization.

WKWebView content process termination recovery for macOS. When macOS kills the WebView render process (memory pressure/idle/sleep), the webview goes white. Hooks into WKNavigationDelegate to auto-reload on webViewWebContentProcessDidTerminate:.

WKWebView first responder management. find_wkwebview uses BFS to locate WKWebView in subview hierarchy. make_webview_first_responder and make_nswindow_webview_first_responder set WKWebView as first responder for keyboard/gesture input.

Frontmost app save/restore for focus management. PREVIOUS_FRONTMOST_APP stores the app pointer before overlay activation. save_frontmost_app, restore_frontmost_app, clear_frontmost_app, and restore_frontmost_app_if_external_with_app manage focus transitions to avoid Space switching.

Pinch-to-zoom and scroll wheel gesture handling for macOS. init_magnify_handler registers ScreenpipeMagnifyHandler (NSMagnificationGestureRecognizer target) and swizzles WKWebView scrollWheel: to emit native-scroll/native-magnify Tauri events. attach_magnify_gesture_to_view attaches gesture recognizer to WKWebView.
Window identifier enum with label(), title(), min_size(), get() methods. Maps to window labels: main, home, search, onboarding, chat, permission-recovery.
Window show request with metadata. Has window_builder_with_label(), window_builder(), id(), metadata(), show(), hide_without_restore(), close(), set_size() methods.

nodes.signature: unsafe fn show_panel_visible(panel: &RawNSPanel, app: &AppHandle, activate_app: bool)
nodes.description: Makes NSPanel visible and interactive on main thread. Sequence: save_frontmost_app -> setAlphaValue(1.0) -> optional activateIgnoringOtherApps -> order_front_regardless -> make_key_window -> make_webview_first_responder -> emit window-focused.

nodes.signature: pub fn with_autorelease_pool<R, F: FnOnce() -> R>(f: F) -> R
nodes.description: Cross-platform autorelease pool wrapper. Uses objc_autoreleasePoolPush/Pop on macOS, no-op on other platforms.

nodes.signature: pub fn run_on_main_thread_safe<F: FnOnce() + Send + 'static>(app: &AppHandle, f: F)
nodes.description: Runs closure on main thread with panic catch_unwind to prevent abort from Rust panics crossing Obj-C FFI boundary. Does NOT create autorelease pool.

nodes.signature: pub fn screen_aware_size(app: &AppHandle, desired_w: f64, desired_h: f64) -> (f64, f64)
nodes.description: Clamps desired window size to 85% of primary monitor dimensions to prevent off-screen windows.
BFS traversal of subview hierarchy from content_view to locate WKWebView. Returns nil if not found.
Finds WKWebView in NSPanel content view and sets it as first responder. Also attaches magnify gesture recognizer. Uses dispatch_async to next runloop tick to avoid race with make_key_window.
For NSWindow (non-panel). Finds WKWebView, disables native scroll on enclosing NSScrollView (vertical/horizontal elasticity none, no scrollers), attaches pinch-to-zoom gesture, sets first responder.
Hooks WKWebView navigation delegate to auto-reload when web content process terminates (macOS memory pressure/idle/sleep). Uses class_replaceMethod to swizzle webViewWebContentProcessDidTerminate:.
Saves current frontmost application before activating screenpipe overlay. Stores as raw pointer (usize) since id is not Send. Retains and releases properly.
Re-activates previously saved frontmost app using NSApplicationActivateIgnoringOtherApps. Releases the stored pointer.
Conditionally restores frontmost app only if focus moved to external app (not another screenpipe window). Checks is_own_app_still_active() and whether Settings/Home/Chat windows are open.

One-time setup for pinch-to-zoom. Registers ScreenpipeMagnifyHandler ObjC class with handleMagnify: method. Also swizzles WKWebView scrollWheel: to emit native-scroll Tauri events with deltaX/deltaY/ctrlKey/metaKey.
Attaches NSMagnificationGestureRecognizer to view. Safe to call multiple times (checks if already attached). Returns early if ScreenpipeMagnifyHandler class not registered yet.

nodes.name: screenpipe-a11y
nodes.description: Cross-platform UI event capture for screenpipe - keyboard, mouse, clipboard, accessibility tree

nodes.name: activity_feed
nodes.description: Lightweight activity feed for adaptive FPS consumers. Provides input activity information WITHOUT event content. Used by screenpipe-vision for adaptive capture rate.

nodes.name: ActivityFeed
nodes.description: Shared activity state with atomic timestamps for last activity, keyboard activity, and keyboard burst count. Provides idle detection and capture parameter recommendations.

nodes.name: ActivityKind
nodes.description: Activity event kind enum: KeyPress, KeyRelease, MouseClick, MouseMove, Scroll

nodes.name: CaptureParams
nodes.description: Recommended capture parameters based on activity level: interval Duration and skip_threshold

nodes.name: budget
nodes.description: Adaptive throttling for accessibility tree walks. Tracks per-app walk cost and automatically backs off for expensive apps.

nodes.name: WalkTier
nodes.description: Throttle tier: Light (<50ms), Moderate (50-150ms), Heavy (150-250ms), Critical (>250ms or repeated truncations)

nodes.name: WalkDecision
nodes.description: Decision returned by AppWalkBudget::should_walk: walk (bool), max_nodes override, timeout override, current tier

nodes.name: AppWalkBudget
nodes.description: Per-app cost tracker with rolling window of recent walk durations, truncation count, and automatic tier escalation/decay

nodes.name: config
nodes.description: Configuration for UI event capture: capture settings, privacy filters, performance tuning, retention settings

nodes.name: UiCaptureConfig
nodes.description: Main config struct with capture settings (clicks, text, keystrokes, app_switch, clipboard, tree), privacy settings (password fields, PII removal, excluded apps), and retention settings

nodes.name: events
nodes.description: UI Event types optimized for storage and AI consumption. Based on bigbrother's event format with extensions.

nodes.name: UiEvent
nodes.description: A UI event with full context: timestamp, relative_ms, EventData (flattened), app_name, window_title, browser_url, element, frame_id

nodes.name: EventData
nodes.description: Tagged union event types: Click, Move, Scroll, Key, Text, AppSwitch, WindowFocus, Clipboard

nodes.name: ElementContext
nodes.description: Element context from accessibility API: role, name, value, description, automation_id, bounds

nodes.name: AccessibilityNode
nodes.description: A node in the accessibility tree with control_type, name, automation_id, class_name, value, bounds, is_enabled, is_focused, is_keyboard_focusable, help_text, is_password, is_selected, is_expanded, accelerator_key, access_key, localized_control_type, children

nodes.name: WindowTreeSnapshot
nodes.description: A full snapshot of a window's accessibility tree: timestamp, app_name, window_title, pid, root AccessibilityNode, element_count, tree_hash

nodes.name: Modifiers
nodes.description: Modifier key flags: SHIFT=1, CTRL=2, OPT=4, CMD=8, CAPS=16, FN=32

nodes.name: incognito
nodes.description: Incognito/private browsing window detection. Combines platform-native queries (macOS AppleScript) with localized title matching across 20+ locales.

nodes.name: IncognitoDetector
nodes.description: Trait for platform-specific incognito detection. Implementations: LinuxIncognitoDetector, MacOSIncognitoDetector, WindowsIncognitoDetector

nodes.name: is_title_private
nodes.description: Pure function checking window title against comprehensive keyword lists: ENGLISH_KEYWORDS (incognito, private browsing, inprivate), LOCALIZED_KEYWORDS (20+ languages), CJK_KEYWORDS (Japanese, Chinese, Korean)

nodes.name: MacOSIncognitoDetector
nodes.description: macOS incognito detector using AppleScript window properties. Queries Chromium browsers (Chrome, Edge, Brave, Vivaldi, Opera) for incognito mode. Falls back to localized title matching.

nodes.name: LinuxIncognitoDetector
nodes.description: Linux incognito detector. Uses localized title matching only (no native API equivalent to macOS AppleScript)

nodes.name: WindowsIncognitoDetector
nodes.description: Windows incognito detector. Uses localized title matching only

nodes.name: platform
nodes.description: Platform-specific UI event capture implementations. Exports UiRecorder, RecordingHandle, PermissionStatus for current platform.

nodes.name: PermissionStatus
nodes.description: Permission status struct with accessibility and input_monitoring bools. all_granted() method.

nodes.name: RecordingHandle
nodes.description: Handle to a running recording session. Methods: stop(), is_running(), receiver(), recv_timeout(). Crossbeam channel-based event delivery.

nodes.name: UiRecorder
nodes.description: UI Event recorder. Methods: new(config), check_permissions(), request_permissions(), start(), start_with_activity_feed(), start_activity_only()

nodes.name: macos
nodes.description: macOS UI event capture using CGEventTap and Accessibility APIs. Uses cidre crate for native macOS APIs.

nodes.name: windows
nodes.description: Windows UI event capture using SetWindowsHookEx and UI Automation. 4-thread architecture: native hooks, app observer, UIA worker, element enrichment.

nodes.name: windows_uia
nodes.description: Windows UI Automation tree capture engine. Uses IUIAutomation COM interfaces with CacheRequest batching and event-driven capture.

nodes.name: UiaContext
nodes.description: UIA context holding COM objects: IUIAutomation, cache_request, walker_cache_request, tree_walker. Lazy initialization with retry logic.

nodes.name: linux
nodes.description: Linux UI event capture using evdev for input and D-Bus AT-SPI2 for window tracking. Uses xdotool for active window detection.

nodes.name: tree
nodes.description: Accessibility tree walker - periodically walks the focused window's accessibility tree, extracts all visible text

nodes.name: TreeWalkerConfig
nodes.description: Config for tree walker: walk_interval, max_depth, max_nodes, walk_timeout, max_text_length, element_timeout_secs, ignored/included_windows, monitor dimensions, incognito ignore, budget overrides

nodes.name: TreeSnapshot
nodes.description: Snapshot of all visible text from focused window's accessibility tree: app_name, window_name, text_content, nodes, browser_url, timestamp, node_count, walk_duration, content_hash, simhash, truncated, truncation_reason, max_depth_reached

nodes.name: TreeWalkResult
nodes.description: Result of tree walk attempt: Found(TreeSnapshot), Skipped(SkipReason), NotFound

nodes.name: SkipReason
nodes.description: Reason window was skipped: Incognito, ExcludedApp, UserIgnored, NotInIncludeList

nodes.name: AccessibilityTreeNode
nodes.description: A single node from accessibility tree: role, text, depth, bounds (NodeBounds), automation_id, class_name, value, help_text, url, placeholder, role_description, subrole, is_enabled, is_focused, is_selected, is_expanded, is_password, is_keyboard_focusable, accelerator_key, access_key

nodes.name: NodeBounds
nodes.description: Bounding box of accessibility node, normalized to 0-1: left, top, width, height

nodes.name: TreeWalkerPlatform
nodes.description: Platform-specific tree walker trait with walk_focused_window() method

nodes.name: create_tree_walker
nodes.description: Creates platform-appropriate tree walker: MacosTreeWalker, WindowsTreeWalker, LinuxTreeWalker

nodes.name: hamming_distance
nodes.description: Computes Hamming distance between two 64-bit hashes (number of differing bits)

nodes.name: cache
nodes.description: Content-hash dedup cache for tree walker snapshots. Uses SimHash with Hamming distance for fuzzy comparison.

nodes.name: TreeCache
nodes.description: Fuzzy-dedup cache keyed on (app_name, window_name). Uses SimHash hamming distance with threshold=10, TTL=60s, MAX_ENTRIES=100

nodes.name: MacosTreeWalker
nodes.description: macOS accessibility tree walker using cidre AX APIs. Uses AXEnhancedUserInterface to trigger Chromium/Electron tree building. Extracts browser URLs via AXDocument, AppleScript (Arc), or shallow AXTextField walk.

nodes.name: WindowsTreeWalker
nodes.description: Windows tree walker using UI Automation. Reuses UiaContext CacheRequest-based capture. Falls back to TreeWalker for apps whose UIA providers don't populate cached subtree (Chromium, Electron).

nodes.name: LinuxTreeWalker
nodes.description: Linux accessibility tree walker using AT-SPI2 via D-Bus. Lazy-connects to AT-SPI bus, enables accessibility for Chromium/Electron apps. Walks focused window with role-based text extraction.

nodes.name: run_uia_thread
nodes.description: UI Automation worker thread: initializes COM, subscribes to focus changes, debounces captures, processes click element requests, uses MsgWaitForMultipleObjects for efficient waiting

nodes.name: ClickElementRequest
nodes.description: Click position request for ElementFromPoint: x, y, timestamp

nodes.label: screenpipe-apple-intelligence
nodes.description: On-device AI processing using Apple Foundation Models framework (macOS 26+). Zero cloud, zero privacy concerns — all processing happens locally on Apple Silicon. Provides Rust bindings to Foundation Models via Swift FFI.

nodes.label: build.rs
nodes.description: Build script that compiles the Swift bridge to a static library. On macOS 26+ SDK: runs swiftc to compile swift/bridge.swift, links FoundationModels.framework (weak-linked), and adds Swift runtime library paths. On older SDKs: creates a stub C library so linking always succeeds.

nodes.label: Cargo.toml
nodes.description: Crate manifest. Features: 'screenpipe-query' (fetches from screenpipe HTTP API), 'server' (OpenAI-compatible Axum server). Dependencies: serde, serde_json, anyhow, chrono, and optionally reqwest, tokio, axum, uuid, tokio-stream.

nodes.label: src/lib.rs
nodes.description: Crate root. Conditionally compiles ffi and engine modules only on aarch64-macos (Apple Silicon). On other platforms the crate is a no-op. Exports engine module via pub use.

nodes.label: src/ffi.rs
nodes.description: Raw FFI declarations for C-callable Swift functions. Declares: fm_check_availability, fm_free_string, fm_generate_text, fm_generate_json, fm_prewarm, fm_supported_languages. All functions are unsafe extern 'C'.

nodes.label: src/engine.rs
nodes.description: Safe Rust API wrappers around FFI. Public functions: check_availability, prewarm, supported_languages, generate_text, generate_json, query_screenpipe_with_ai (feature=screenpipe-query), analyze_app_usage_time (feature=screenpipe-query). Types: Availability enum, GenerationMetrics, GenerationResult, JsonGenerationResult, AppUsageEntry, FrameTimestamp. Utility: compute_app_usage, format_app_usage_for_prompt. 1381 lines with comprehensive tests.

nodes.label: src/server_bin.rs
nodes.description: OpenAI-compatible Axum HTTP server (fm-server binary). Endpoints: POST /v1/chat/completions, POST /v1/extract-todos (dedicated TODO extraction with pre-filtering and deduplication), GET /v1/models, GET /health. Includes ACTION_KEYWORDS pre-filter list (70+ keywords), chunk_text, dedup_todos, prefilter_for_actions. Server runs on port 5273 by default.

nodes.label: swift/bridge.swift
nodes.description: Swift FFI bridge compiled by build.rs. Wraps Apple FoundationModels framework (LanguageModelSession, SystemLanguageModel). Implements: fmCheckAvailability, fmGenerateText, fmGenerateJson, fmPrewarm, fmSupportedLanguages. Uses DispatchSemaphore to convert async Swift Concurrency to sync C FFI. Measures memory via mach_task_basic_info. Target: arm64-apple-macos14.0. All FoundationModels usage guarded by @available(macOS 26, *).

nodes.label: SystemLanguageModel (Apple)
nodes.description: Apple's Foundation Models framework class. Accessed as SystemLanguageModel.default. Provides .availability property (available/unavailable cases: appleIntelligenceNotEnabled, deviceNotEligible, modelNotReady). Used to create LanguageModelSession for text generation.

nodes.label: LanguageModelSession (Apple)
nodes.description: Apple's Foundation Models session class. Created with optional instructions. Supports respond(to:prompt) for plain text and respond(to:prompt, schema:) for JSON-constrained output. Has prewarm() method for pre-loading model assets.

nodes.label: fm-server binary
nodes.description: Standalone HTTP server binary exposing Apple Foundation Models via OpenAI-compatible API. Built from src/server_bin.rs. Requires 'server' feature. Start with: cargo run --features server --bin fm-server.

nodes.label: Availability (Rust enum)
nodes.description: Enum representing Foundation Models availability status: Available, AppleIntelligenceNotEnabled, DeviceNotEligible, ModelNotReady, Unknown(String). Returned by check_availability().

nodes.label: GenerationMetrics (Rust struct)
nodes.description: Performance metrics struct: total_time_ms, mem_before_bytes, mem_after_bytes, mem_delta_bytes. Returned as part of GenerationResult and JsonGenerationResult.

nodes.label: GenerationResult (Rust struct)
nodes.description: Result of a text generation request: text (String) and metrics (GenerationMetrics).

nodes.label: JsonGenerationResult (Rust struct)
nodes.description: Result of a JSON generation request: json (serde_json::Value) and metrics (GenerationMetrics).

nodes.label: TodoItem (Rust struct)
nodes.description: A TODO/action item extracted from screen/audio: text (String), app (Option<String>), urgency (String: high/medium/low). Used by the /v1/extract-todos endpoint.

nodes.label: AppUsageEntry (Rust struct)
nodes.description: Time spent in an application: app_name, duration_secs, percentage, frame_count. Produced by compute_app_usage().

nodes.label: FrameTimestamp (Rust struct)
nodes.description: Minimal frame data for usage computation: timestamp (ISO 8601 string), app_name. Input to compute_app_usage().

nodes.label: ExtractRequest (Rust struct)
nodes.description: HTTP request body for /v1/extract-todos: chunks (Vec<String>), chunk_size (usize, default 1200).

nodes.label: ExtractResponse (Rust struct)
nodes.description: HTTP response from /v1/extract-todos: items (Vec<TodoItem>), stats (ExtractStats with chunks_processed, chunks_with_results, total_input_chars, filtered_input_chars, total_time_ms, pre_filter_ratio).

nodes.label: ChatCompletionRequest (Rust struct)
nodes.description: OpenAI-compatible chat completion request: model, messages, stream, temperature, max_tokens, tools, tool_choice. Supported by /v1/chat/completions.

nodes.label: check_availability()
nodes.description: Public API: Check if Apple Foundation Models is available. Returns Availability enum. Calls FFI fm_check_availability after checking macOS version >= 26.

nodes.label: generate_text()
nodes.description: Public API: Generate plain text response. Takes optional instructions string and a prompt. Returns GenerationResult with text and metrics. Calls FFI fm_generate_text.

nodes.label: generate_json()
nodes.description: Public API: Generate JSON constrained by a schema. Takes optional instructions, prompt, and JSON schema string. Returns JsonGenerationResult. Calls FFI fm_generate_json.

nodes.label: prewarm()
nodes.description: Public API: Pre-warm the Foundation Models engine by loading model assets. Blocking call. Calls FFI fm_prewarm.

nodes.label: supported_languages()
nodes.description: Public API: Get list of languages supported by the on-device model. Returns Vec<String>. Calls FFI fm_supported_languages.

nodes.label: query_screenpipe_with_ai()
nodes.description: Public API (screenpipe-query feature): Fetches recent OCR and audio data from screenpipe HTTP API (localhost:port), builds a context prompt, and sends to generate_text. Async function.

nodes.label: analyze_app_usage_time()
nodes.description: Public API (screenpipe-query feature): Fetches OCR frames from screenpipe, computes time per app via compute_app_usage, formats summary, and sends to generate_text for AI analysis. Async function.

nodes.label: compute_app_usage()
nodes.description: Compute time spent per application from frame timestamps. Algorithm: sort frames, assign each inter-frame gap to current app, cap gaps at max_gap_secs (default 300s). Returns Vec<AppUsageEntry> sorted by duration descending.

nodes.label: format_app_usage_for_prompt()
nodes.description: Format app usage entries into a compact text string for AI prompts. Skips apps with < 30s duration. Includes total tracked time and top 30 apps.

nodes.label: prefilter_for_actions()
nodes.description: Server utility: Pre-filter text to keep only lines likely to contain action items using 70+ ACTION_KEYWORDS. Keeps matched line plus 1 line of context before/after. Critical for reducing volume 10-50x for the ~3B model with ~2000 token context.

nodes.label: chunk_text()
nodes.description: Server utility: Split text into chunks with ~15% overlap for boundary coverage. Used by /v1/extract-todos to handle texts larger than chunk_size.

nodes.label: dedup_todos()
nodes.description: Server utility: Deduplicate TODO items by normalized text similarity. Uses lowercase alphanumeric comparison and substring checking for near-duplicates.

nodes.label: extract_todos (HTTP handler)
nodes.description: HTTP handler for POST /v1/extract-todos. Steps: pre-filter chunks for action keywords, re-chunk filtered text, process each chunk with generate_text using TODO_INSTRUCTIONS, parse JSON response, deduplicate, return ExtractResponse with stats.

nodes.label: chat_completions (HTTP handler)
nodes.description: HTTP handler for POST /v1/chat/completions. OpenAI-compatible. Builds prompt from messages, calls generate_text, optionally parses tool_call blocks from response. Supports streaming and non-streaming.

nodes.label: is_macos_26_or_later()
nodes.description: Internal helper: Checks if running on macOS 26+ by running 'sw_vers -productVersion'. Returns false on non-macOS. Used to gate all FFI calls.

nodes.label: extract_and_free()
nodes.description: Internal unsafe helper: Extract a Rust String from a C char pointer and free the C memory via fm_free_string. Used by all FFI call wrappers.

nodes.label: GenerationSchema (Swift)
nodes.description: Swift type used by Apple's Foundation Models to constrain JSON output. Decoded from JSON string in generate_json. The Swift side uses this to call session.respond(to:prompt, schema:).

nodes.name: transcription
nodes.description: Core transcription module for screenpipe-audio. Provides audio-to-text transcription via multiple STT engines (Whisper, Deepgram, OpenAI-compatible, Qwen3-ASR, Parakeet). Handles audio segment processing, VAD, speaker embedding, vocabulary biasing, and PII removal.

nodes.name: stt (orchestration)
nodes.description: Main STT orchestration layer. Provides AlternateStt trait for custom engines, OpenAICompatibleConfig for HTTP transcription servers, stt() async function that dispatches to selected engine (Deepgram/Whisper/Qwen3-ASR/Parakeet/OpenAI-compatible), process_audio_input() for full pipeline (VAD -> segments -> transcription), and run_stt() for per-segment transcription.

nodes.name: text_utils
nodes.description: Text processing utilities for transcription overlap cleanup. Provides longest_common_word_substring() using dynamic programming to find longest common word-sequence between two strings, used for deduplication across audio segments.

nodes.name: TranscriptionEngine + TranscriptionSession
nodes.description: Unified transcription engine enum (Whisper/Deepgram/OpenAI-compatible/Qwen3-ASR/Parakeet/Disabled) and per-thread TranscriptionSession. Factory new() loads only the needed model. create_session() spawns per-thread sessions. Session transcribe() dispatches to backend and applies vocabulary post-processing.

nodes.name: handle_new_transcript
nodes.description: Async task that consumes TranscriptionResult from channel and inserts into DB. Maintains per-device previous transcript for overlap cleanup. Applies PII removal if enabled. Emits AudioInsertInfo callbacks for hot-frame cache.

nodes.name: TranscriptionResult
nodes.description: TranscriptionResult struct and process_transcription_result() for DB insertion. Includes cleanup_overlap() for deduplication, get_or_create_speaker_from_embedding() for speaker identification, and PII removal (email/SSN/credit card redaction) via screenpipe_core::pii_removal.

nodes.name: transcribe_with_deepgram
nodes.description: Deepgram API transcription. Encodes audio as MP3 (64kbps mono), builds query params with model=nova-3, smart_format, language detection, and vocabulary keywords (up to 100). Handles network errors (firewall/proxy/captive portal detection). Falls back to Whisper on failure.

nodes.name: deepgram config
nodes.description: Deepgram endpoint configuration via environment variables: DEEPGRAM_API_URL (default: api.deepgram.com/v1/listen) and CUSTOM_DEEPGRAM_API_TOKEN for proxy endpoints.

nodes.name: transcribe_with_openai_compatible
nodes.description: OpenAI-compatible HTTP transcription API client. Sends audio as MP3 or raw WAV to any /v1/audio/transcriptions endpoint. Supports custom headers, API key auth (bearer), vocabulary as prompt+context fields, and language hints. Used for local Whisper servers, mlx-audio, llama.cpp, vLLM backends.

nodes.name: openai_compatible module
nodes.description: OpenAI-compatible module entry point. Exports batch submodule with transcribe_with_openai_compatible function.

nodes.name: process_with_whisper
nodes.description: Whisper.cpp transcription via whisper_rs. Pre-checks RMS energy threshold (0.015) to skip near-silence and prevent hallucinations. Uses greedy sampling, suppresses blank/nst tokens, entropy threshold 2.4, logprob threshold -2.0. Applies vocabulary as initial_prompt (truncated to 800 chars). Detects language via lang_detect().

nodes.name: detect_language
nodes.description: Language detection from Whisper token probabilities using whisper_rs get_lang_str/get_lang_str_full. Returns first detected language that matches the user's language preference list.

nodes.name: whisper model utilities
nodes.description: Whisper model download (from ggerganov/whisper.cpp HuggingFace repo) and context creation. Model variants: ggml-tiny.bin, ggml-tiny-q8_0.bin, ggml-large-v3.bin, ggml-large-v3-q5_0.bin, ggml-large-v3-turbo.bin. GPU acceleration enabled (Metal on macOS, Vulkan on Windows). DTW disabled to avoid median_filter asserts.

nodes.name: whisper module
nodes.description: Whisper transcription module re-exporting detect_language and model submodules.

nodes.name: AudioInput
nodes.description: Audio data container with data (Arc<Vec<f32>>), sample_rate, channels, device, and capture_timestamp (epoch seconds for correct timeline placement in deferred processing).

nodes.name: VocabularyEntry
nodes.description: Custom vocabulary entry for transcription biasing. word is the term to bias toward, replacement is optionally substituted in post-processing. Serialized as JSON for config.

nodes.name: TranscriptionResult
nodes.description: Result of audio transcription containing path, input AudioInput, speaker_embedding, transcription text, timestamp, error, start_time, end_time. cleanup_overlap() removes duplicate content between consecutive segments using word-level LCS.

nodes.name: AudioInsertResult
nodes.description: Result of successful audio transcription DB insert, containing audio_chunk_id and optional speaker_id.

nodes.name: AudioInsertInfo
nodes.description: Callback payload after audio transcription DB insert. Includes audio_chunk_id, transcription, device_name, is_input, audio_file_path, duration_secs, start_time, end_time, speaker_id, capture_timestamp.

nodes.name: AudioInsertCallback
nodes.description: Callback type: Arc<dyn Fn(AudioInsertInfo) + Send + Sync> for post-insert notifications (e.g., hot frame cache).

nodes.name: OpenAICompatibleConfig
nodes.description: Configuration for OpenAI-compatible transcription: endpoint URL, optional API key, model name, optional reqwest Client (for connection pooling), custom HTTP headers, and raw_audio flag (WAV vs MP3). Defaults to 127.0.0.1:8080 with whisper-1 model.

nodes.name: AlternateStt
nodes.description: Trait for alternate STT engines (Qwen3-ASR, Parakeet via audiopipe). Method: transcribe(&mut self, audio: &[f32], sample_rate: u32) -> Result<String>. Uses trait object (Box<dyn AlternateStt + Send>) for compile-time flexibility across feature flags.

nodes.name: TranscriptionEngine
nodes.description: Unified engine enum: Whisper (with context/config/languages/vocabulary), Qwen3Asr, Parakeet, ParakeetMlx, Deepgram, OpenAICompatible, Disabled. Factory new() async loads only the selected engine's model. Variants are feature-gated (qwen3-asr, parakeet, parakeet-mlx).

nodes.name: TranscriptionSession
nodes.description: Per-thread transcription session holding engine-specific state: WhisperState ( !Send) for Whisper, Arc<Mutex<audiopipe::Model>> for Qwen3-ASR/Parakeet, api_key for Deepgram, endpoint/client for OpenAI-compatible. transcribe() async method dispatches to appropriate backend and applies vocabulary post-processing.

nodes.name: SAMPLE_RATE = 16000
nodes.description: Target audio sample rate for transcription (16kHz, standard for Whisper and most STT APIs).

nodes.name: DEFAULT_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_ENDPOINT
nodes.description: Default endpoint for OpenAI-compatible transcription: http://127.0.0.1:8080

nodes.name: DEFAULT_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_MODEL
nodes.description: Default model name for OpenAI-compatible transcription: whisper-1

nodes.name: OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_TIMEOUT_SECS = 30
nodes.description: Timeout in seconds for OpenAI-compatible transcription HTTP requests.

nodes.name: MIN_RMS_ENERGY = 0.015
nodes.description: Minimum RMS energy threshold for Whisper transcription. Below this, audio is near-silent and Whisper hallucinates. Value calibrated against silence (0.0), ambient noise (~0.007), white noise (~0.071), normal speech (~0.05-0.3).

nodes.name: VadEngine
nodes.description: Trait for voice activity detection engines. Implementations: WebRtcVad, SileroVad.

nodes.name: VadEngineEnum
nodes.description: VAD engine selection: WebRtc or Silero.

nodes.name: SileroVad
nodes.description: Silero VAD implementation using vad-rs crate. Supports VAD status detection and speech probability thresholds. 3x faster than WebRTC, supports 6000+ languages.

nodes.name: WebRtcVad
nodes.description: WebRTC VAD implementation using webrtc-vad crate. Cross-platform voice activity detection.

nodes.name: process_audio_input
nodes.description: Processes audio input through VAD, segmentation, embedding, and transcription pipeline.

nodes.name: stt
nodes.description: Speech-to-text function dispatching to appropriate engine (Whisper, Deepgram, OpenAI-compatible, Qwen3, Parakeet).

nodes.name: process_transcription_result
nodes.description: Processes transcription result: PII removal, speaker matching/creation, DB insert with retry logic.

nodes.name: reconciliation
nodes.description: Batch mode reconciliation: transcribes orphaned audio chunks after meeting/call ends.

nodes.name: accuracy_test
nodes.description: Transcription accuracy test using ground truth transcriptions for test_data/accuracy{1-5}.wav files.

nodes.description: Test audio for transcription accuracy testing.

data.name: logging
data.description: Size-aware rolling log file writer with per-file size limits and total-size cap with automatic cleanup. Implements std::io::Write for use with tracing_appender::non_blocking.

data.name: meeting_persister
data.description: DEPRECATED stub. Meeting detection loop handles its own DB persistence directly. Module kept as empty placeholder.

data.name: meeting_detector
data.description: Meeting detection v2 via UI element scanning. Periodically scans accessibility tree of meeting app processes for call control elements (mute, leave, hangup buttons). Replaces app-focus-based detection.

data.name: analytics
data.description: PostHog telemetry integration. Captures analytics events (api_usage, macOS version checks) asynchronously with configurable telemetry enable/disable.

data.name: apple_intelligence_api
data.description: OpenAI-compatible API wrapping Apple Foundation Models. Supports tool calling, JSON schema mode, and streaming. Feature-gated behind apple-intelligence flag (macOS aarch64 only).

data.name: archive
data.description: Cloud Archive: encrypts and uploads old data to cloud then deletes locally. Uses watermark-based cleanup (archive_uploaded_through) instead of per-record synced_at tracking.

data.name: auto_destruct
data.description: Process termination watcher. Monitors if a managed app process exits (e.g. screenpipe-app) and signals shutdown. Uses platform APIs: Windows OpenProcess/GetExitCodeProcess or Unix libc::kill(pid, 0).

data.name: calendar_speaker_id
data.description: Calendar-assisted speaker identification. Uses calendar meeting context (attendees) + input/output device signals to automatically name unnamed speakers. Pure decision logic with comprehensive unit tests.

data.name: cloud_search
data.description: Hybrid local + cloud search queries. CloudSearchClient manages encrypted cloud data search with status tracking (Available/Disabled/NotInitialized).

data.name: connections_api
data.description: HTTP API for connection credential management (integrations: calendar, WhatsApp, etc.). Exposes native OS calendar via EventKit (macOS) and WinRT (Windows).

data.name: drm_detector
data.description: DRM content detection — pauses screen capture when streaming apps (Netflix, Disney+, etc.) are focused. Uses only Accessibility APIs (not ScreenCaptureKit) for pre-capture gate checks. Multi-monitor aware.

data.name: event_driven_capture
data.description: Event-triggered capture loop replacing polling-based continuous capture. Captures on: app switch, window focus, click, typing pause, scroll stop, clipboard, visual change, idle fallback. Uses ActivityFeed for state transitions.

data.name: hot_frame_cache
data.description: In-memory cache for today's frames and audio. Uses BTreeMap for O(log n) insert and time-range queries. Broadcast channels push live updates to WS handlers. Day-rollover clears cache.

data.name: screenpipe_engine
data.description: Main library entry point. Re-exports all public modules, types, and builder functions (RecordingConfig, start_meeting_watcher, start_power_manager, etc.).

data.name: meeting_watcher
data.description: Spawns the meeting detector UI-scanning loop. Thin wrapper around meeting_detector::run_meeting_detection_loop passing the MeetingDetector for audio pipeline override.

data.name: paired_capture
data.description: Paired screenshot + accessibility tree walk in one atomic operation. Core of event-driven capture. Handles OCR fallback, PII removal, content dedup, and canvas/thin-a11y heuristics.

data.name: pipe_permissions_middleware
data.description: Axum middleware enforcing pipe API permissions. Validates Bearer sp_pipe_* tokens against PipePermissions registry. Implements deny→allow→default allowlist→reject logic.

data.name: pipe_store
data.description: SQLite-backed PipeStore for persisting pipe execution history. Uses screenpipe_db write queue for serialized writes to avoid WAL contention. Implements PipeStore trait from screenpipe_core.

data.name: pipes_api
