Tests for consumer loop sleep behavior bug fix. Compares old consumer (sleeps 1/fps every iteration) vs new consumer (only sleeps when queue empty). Uses ArrayQueue producer-consumer simulation with burst FPS testing.
Simulates OLD consumer behavior: sleep(1/fps) every iteration regardless of queue state. Caused frame drops during adaptive FPS bursts (10 FPS) because queue backed up.
Simulates NEW consumer behavior: only sleeps 50ms when queue empty. Processes frames immediately when available. Fixes frame drops during burst conditions.
Simulates adaptive FPS burst producer. Pushes frames at burst_fps for burst_duration. Tracks dropped frames when queue (30 slots) overflows.
Verifies old consumer loses frames during 10 FPS burst with 0.5 FPS base setting. Old consumer processes ~4 frames in 8s vs 50 produced.
Verifies new consumer drains immediately and does not drop frames during 10 FPS burst. All frames processed with zero drops.
Verifies new consumer sleeps 50ms per iteration when queue empty, not spinning at 100% CPU.
Verifies new consumer processes frames without delay when available. 5 frames processed in <10ms then idle sleep-polling.
Verifies new consumer handles steady 0.5 FPS (1 frame/2s) trivially with no drops.
Integration tests for SCServer REST API endpoints using Axum Router. Tests search, count, time constraints with in-memory SQLite DB.
Creates test SCServer with in-memory SQLite DB and AudioManager for endpoint testing.
Tests /search endpoint with min_length and max_length query params for audio content. Ignored - requires Silero VAD ONNX models.
Tests count_search_results with ContentType filters (All/OCR/Audio), app_name, window_name, and length constraints.
Tests /search with start_time and end_time constraints for OCR and audio. Uses RFC3339 timestamps with 2-hour offset.
Tests 30-second window search returns only recent task 'fix bug #123' and not old task 'write documentation' from 4 hours ago.
Tests with real ~/.screenpipe/db.sqlite (read-only mode) verifying no data bleeding between time windows.
End-to-end test for screen capture to API delay. Polls /search endpoint until 'without a garbage collector' phrase found from Wikipedia Rust page. Measures total latency from recording to searchable.
Measures end-to-end delay from screen recording to searchable via API. Polls localhost:3030/search. Mac M3 max: ~33-153 seconds.
Tests for diagnosing 'first frames not loading' issue. Uses real screenpipe DB and tests frame extraction with diagnostics.
Fetches 10 most recent frames and attempts extraction. Reports success/failure with file existence, extraction results, timeout. Requires >=80% success rate.
Loads 20 frames concurrently (simulates timeline scrolling). Requires >=80% success rate.
Tests /frames/:id endpoint response times for 5 recent frames via HTTP client.
Checks if video files from last 5 minutes are still being written (modified <10s ago warning).
Tests for moov atom issue in MP4 frame extraction. Tests incomplete vs complete MP4, fragmented MP4 (fix), and race conditions.
Finds ffmpeg binary in common locations: /opt/homebrew/bin/ffmpeg, /usr/local/bin/ffmpeg, /usr/bin/ffmpeg, ffmpeg.
Creates incomplete MP4 by spawning ffmpeg that writes to file. MP4 moov atom only at end so incomplete files can't be read.
Extracts single frame from video using ffmpeg -ss 0 -vframes 1. Returns error if extraction fails.
Creates MP4 with ftyp box only (no moov), tries extraction. Expects 'moov atom not found' or 'Invalid data' error. Reproduces SCREENPIPE-CLI-D/X/T.
Creates complete valid MP4 with ffmpeg, verifies frame extraction works.
THE FIX TEST: Creates fragmented MP4 with -movflags frag_keyframe+empty_moov+default_base_moof. Verifies frame extraction works WHILE recording is in progress.
Compares regular MP4 vs fragmented MP4 file sizes. Fragmented should be <2x size of regular.
Verifies fragmented MP4 is valid H.265 with ffprobe, tests seeking (multiple frame extractions at different timestamps).
Simulates race condition: starts ffmpeg recording, immediately tries frame extraction. Demonstrates timing issue.
Unit tests for health check debouncing logic from apps/screenpipe-app-tauri health.rs. Tests RecordingStatus state machine with grace period and failure threshold.
Recording health status: Starting, Recording, Stopped, Error.
Core health check decision logic. 30s startup grace period, 3 consecutive failures threshold for transition to Stopped. Explicit unhealthy bypasses debounce.
Healthy response always returns Recording status.
Unhealthy response always returns Error status.
Connection error during 30s grace period returns Starting status.
Connection error after grace period (>30s) returns Stopped status.
Single failure while recording does NOT flip to Stopped (debounce anti-flicker).
Two failures while recording still holds Recording (threshold is 3).
Third consecutive failure while recording transitions to Stopped.
Simulates flicker: healthy -> timeout -> healthy -> 2 timeouts -> healthy. Verifies no flickering through sequence.
WebSocket frame streaming tests for /stream/frames endpoint. Tests new frame push, multiple clients, time filtering, reconnection.
WebSocket request format for stream frames: start_time, end_time, order.
BUG REPRODUCTION: Verifies new frames inserted after initial fetch are NOT pushed to WebSocket client. Confirms timeline refresh bug.
Tests that multiple WebSocket clients all receive new frames.
Verifies only frames within requested time range are returned.
Tests reconnection receives fresh data (may have more frames if recorded between connections).
Tests empty result for far future time range.
Integration tests for /tags API endpoints. Tests POST add tags, DELETE remove tags, search includes tags for OCR and audio content.
Helper to insert test OCR and audio data with Tesseract engine and AudioDevice.
Tests POST /tags/vision/:id and /tags/audio/:id, verifies search results include tags.
Tests adding multiple tags ['work', 'meeting', 'important', 'follow-up'] to single item.
Tests DELETE /tags/:content_type/:id removes specified tags but keeps others.
Tests searching with tags filter for multiple content types.
Tests for timeline refresh bug where new frames not pushed to WebSocket clients. Simulates polling logic, channel pattern fix, screenpipe app filtering.
Simulates fetch_new_frames_since logic: finds video chunks, skips already-sent frames, returns new frame IDs and latest timestamp.
BUG CHECK: Verifies frame inserted AFTER client connected IS detected by polling. Core bug reproduction.
Verifies already-sent frames (in HashSet) are correctly excluded from poll results.
Verifies polling correctly filters to only frames within requested time range.
THE FIX: Tests Option-wrapped channel pattern prevents poll timer starvation after channel close. Verified poll runs >=5 times.
Verifies polling condition 'now <= end_time' works for today's request (end_time 23:59:59).
Reproduces exact user bug: timeline shows 7:41 PM but current time 7:55 PM (14 min gap). Should detect 7:43 and 7:45 PM frames.
Tests should_include_entry filters out 'screenpipe'/'Screenpipe'/'SCREENPIPE'/'screenpipe-app' app names.
THE FIX: Frames with empty frame_data (all screenpipe entries filtered) should NOT be sent. Fixes 'Unknown' display bug.
Tests orphaned frame detection: video file missing returns graceful error.
Tests for FrameCache in video_cache.rs. Tests JPEG integrity, retrieval at different times, cache effectiveness (2x speedup), cleanup.
Creates FrameCache with real screenpipe data dir and DatabaseManager.
Helper that measures frame retrieval time and count with 30s timeout.
Verifies frame has valid JPEG header (0xFF 0xD8) and footer (0xFF 0xD9), size between 10KB-1MB.
Tests frame retrieval at current, 5min, 30min, 4h, 1day, 2days, 1week ago. Measures FPS performance.
THE KEY TEST: Verifies 2nd request is >=2x faster than 1st (cached). Also verifies frame data integrity between requests.
Verifies frames returned in descending order (newest first).
Verifies frames within 7-day retention period are kept, older frames are removed.
Tests for video_utils (extract_frames_from_video, get_video_metadata). Uses real screenpipe data dir, tests OCR on extracted frames.
Finds first video file containing 'monitor' in screenpipe data dir.
Tests extract_frames_from_video with write_to_disk true and false. Verifies frame dimensions.
Tests frame extraction + Apple OCR (macOS). Verifies text extraction and confidence >0.
Tests get_video_metadata returns valid fps>0, duration>0, creation_time<=now.
Simple WebSocket test for /ws/events endpoint. Connects, sends test event, waits for response.
Connects to ws://127.0.0.1:3030/ws/events, sends test_event JSON, waits for response.

nodes.label: screenpipe-screen
nodes.description: Core screen capture and OCR crate for screenpipe. Handles monitor enumeration, frame comparison, window capture, and OCR processing across macOS, Windows, and Linux.

nodes.label: SafeMonitor
nodes.description: Cross-platform monitor wrapper. Caches native monitor handles to avoid re-enumeration. Supports both sck-rs (ScreenCaptureKit) and xcap backends on macOS, xcap on other platforms.

nodes.label: MonitorData
nodes.description: Monitor metadata including dimensions, position, name, and primary status.

nodes.label: MonitorListError
nodes.description: Error type distinguishing permission denied from other monitor listing failures.

nodes.label: WindowOcrCache
nodes.description: LRU cache for OCR results keyed by window_id + image_hash. Prevents re-OCR of unchanged windows. Configurable max_age and max_entries.

nodes.label: WindowCacheKey
nodes.description: Cache key combining window_id (app_name::window_name) and image_hash for OCR result lookup.

nodes.label: CachedOcrResult
nodes.description: Cached OCR result with text, JSON, confidence, and timestamp.

nodes.label: FrameComparer
nodes.description: Stateful frame comparison with hash-based early exit and downscaled histogram comparison. CPU-optimized for multi-monitor setups.

nodes.label: FrameComparisonConfig
nodes.description: Configuration for frame comparison optimizations: hash_early_exit, downscale_comparison, downscale_factor, single_metric.

nodes.label: perform_ocr_apple
nodes.description: macOS Vision OCR using CoreML/Vision framework. Uses Vision's RecognizeTextRequest. Returns (text, json, confidence). Handles zero-dimension images safely.

nodes.label: get_apple_languages
nodes.description: Maps screenpipe_core::Language to Apple Vision language codes (en-US, zh-Hans, etc).

nodes.label: perform_ocr_tesseract
nodes.description: Tesseract OCR for Linux. Uses rusty_tesseract with PSM 1 and OEM 1. Returns (text, json, confidence).

nodes.label: perform_ocr_windows
nodes.description: Windows native OCR using Windows.Media.Ocr. Uses OcrEngine::TryCreateFromUserProfileLanguages. Returns (text, json, confidence).

nodes.label: perform_ocr_custom
nodes.description: Custom external OCR API. Sends base64 JPEG to configurable endpoint with API key auth. Configurable timeout.

nodes.label: CustomOcrConfig
nodes.description: Configuration for custom OCR: api_url, api_key, timeout_ms.

nodes.label: OcrEngine
nodes.description: OCR engine selection: Unstructured, Tesseract, WindowsNative, AppleNative, Custom. Has platform_default() and FromStr impl.

nodes.label: CapturedWindow
nodes.description: Captured window data: image, app_name, window_name, process_id, is_focused, browser_url, and window coordinates for OCR coordinate transformation.

nodes.label: WindowFilters
nodes.description: Window filtering for privacy (banking sites) and app exclusion. Supports ignore_list, include_list, and ignored_urls. Domain-level URL matching.

nodes.label: BrowserUrlDetector
nodes.description: Trait for detecting active browser URLs. Implemented per-platform: MacOSUrlDetector, WindowsUrlDetector, LinuxUrlDetector.

nodes.label: MacOSUrlDetector
nodes.description: macOS URL detection using AXAccessibility API and AppleScript for Arc. Cross-checks title to detect tab switches during capture.

nodes.label: WindowsUrlDetector
nodes.description: Windows URL detection using UIAutomation to find address bar Edit control.

nodes.label: LinuxUrlDetector
nodes.description: Linux URL detection using AT-SPI2 accessibility bus and DBus.

nodes.label: strip_title_badge
nodes.description: Strips notification counts from browser tab titles: (45), [2], emoji badges. Used for title matching.

nodes.label: titles_match
nodes.description: Compares two browser window titles accounting for badge counts and formatting differences.

nodes.label: continuous_capture
nodes.description: Main capture loop. Captures monitor, compares frames, skips unchanged frames. Sends RawCaptureResult to OCR worker.

nodes.label: process_ocr_task
nodes.description: OCR processing task. Checks cache, performs OCR on miss, caches results. Returns OcrTaskResult with cache stats.

nodes.label: RawCaptureResult
nodes.description: Raw capture result: image, window_images, frame_number, timestamp, captured_at.

nodes.label: CaptureResult
nodes.description: Final capture result with OCR: image, frame_number, timestamp, captured_at, window_ocr_results.

nodes.label: WindowOcrResult
nodes.description: OCR result for a window: window_name, app_name, text, text_json, focused, confidence, browser_url.

nodes.label: RealtimeVisionEvent
nodes.description: Event enum for real-time vision: Ocr(WindowOcr).

nodes.label: WindowOcr
nodes.description: Real-time OCR data with optional image, window/app names, text, JSON, confidence, timestamp.

nodes.label: PipelineMetrics
nodes.description: Thread-safe metrics: frames_captured, frames_skipped, ocr_completed, cache_hits/misses, DB/video queue depths, stall counts. Uses atomic counters.

nodes.label: MetricsSnapshot
nodes.description: Point-in-time metrics snapshot for reporting: uptime, FPS, latency, drop rate, queue depths.

nodes.label: SnapshotWriter
nodes.description: JPEG snapshot writer for event-driven capture. Writes to ~/.screenpipe/data/YYYY-MM-DD/. Supports cleanup of old snapshots.

nodes.label: PersistentCapture
nodes.description: Windows WGC persistent capture session. Keeps GraphicsCaptureSession alive to avoid orange border flash. Consumer thread pattern.

nodes.label: Rect
nodes.description: Rectangle for overlap calculations in window-to-monitor matching. Used for multi-monitor support.

nodes.label: CGWindowInfo
nodes.description: macOS window info from CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo: pid, layer, bounds, owner_name, window_name.

nodes.label: find_topmost_pid_on_monitor
nodes.description: macOS: Finds topmost normal (layer 0) window on a monitor using CGWindowList z-order. Skips overlay apps.

nodes.label: get_frontmost_pid
nodes.description: macOS: Queries frontmost app PID once per capture cycle for consistent focus detection.

nodes.label: capture_all_visible_windows
nodes.description: Captures all visible windows on a monitor. Uses platform-specific backends (sck-rs, xcap). Handles focus detection and overlay filtering.

nodes.label: capture_monitor_image
nodes.description: Captures monitor screenshot without window capture. Defers window capture until after frame comparison.

nodes.label: compare_images_histogram
nodes.description: Compares images using Hellinger histogram metric via image_compare crate.

nodes.label: compare_images_ssim
nodes.description: Compares images using MSSIM structural similarity metric.

nodes.label: calculate_hash
nodes.description: Computes DefaultHasher hash of image bytes for frame comparison early exit.

nodes.label: compare_histogram
nodes.description: Public histogram comparison function for frame comparison.

nodes.label: compare_ssim
nodes.description: Public SSIM comparison function for frame comparison.

nodes.label: calculate_image_hash
nodes.description: Hash function for frame comparison. Operates on downscaled images.

nodes.label: transform_ocr_coordinates_to_screen
nodes.description: Transforms OCR coordinates from window-relative (0-1) to screen-relative (0-1) for consistent DB storage.

nodes.label: ocr_benchmark
nodes.description: Criterion benchmark for OCR engines: perform_ocr_apple, perform_ocr_tesseract, perform_ocr_windows. Measures accuracy and performance.

nodes.label: apple_leak_bench
nodes.description: Memory leak detection benchmark for Apple Vision OCR. Monitors physical memory over 100+ iterations.

nodes.label: frame_comparison_bench
nodes.description: Criterion benchmark comparing original vs optimized frame comparison: hash early exit, downscaling, single metric.

nodes.label: multi_monitor_bench
nodes.description: Multi-monitor performance benchmarks: hash computation, downscale strategies, full pipeline cost, memory allocation. Tests realistic setups (1080p, 1440p, 4K, ultrawide).

nodes.title: For Developers
nodes.description: Give AI coding assistants memory of your screen

Voice input/output handler. Supports: (1) transcription via handleVoiceTranscription, (2) voice query (audio+AI text) via handleVoiceQuery, (3) text-to-speech via handleTextToSpeech, (4) voice chat (audio in, audio out) via handleVoiceChat. Validates audio input, supports multiple TTS voice models (aura-asteria-en through aura-zeus-en).
TODO stub. Contains only a TODO comment, no implementation.
Web search handler using Gemini's Google Search grounding. Accepts {query} JSON body, validates query is non-empty, requires GEMINI_API_KEY, returns {query, content, sources}.

Chat completions handler with auto model waterfall routing. Supports 'auto' model that falls through free models (glm-5, kimi-k2.5, glm-4.7, qwen3.5-flash, gemini-3-flash) until one succeeds. Vision-capable waterfall uses llama-4-maverick, gemini-3-flash, llama-4-scout. Injects screenpipe system hint into messages. Logs model outcomes (ok/rate_limited/error) to model-health service.

Model listing handler with curated catalog (CURATED_MODELS). Returns rich metadata per model: id, owned_by, context_window, speed, intelligence, cost_tier, recommended_for, warning, live health status. Supports tier-based filtering (anonymous, logged_in, subscribed) via usage-tracker. Models include: auto, GLM-4.7, GLM-5, Kimi K2.5, Llama 4 Maverick/Scout, Gemini 3 Flash, Claude Opus/Sonnet/Haiku 4.x, Qwen3.5 Flash, DeepSeek V3.2, Gemini 3.1 Pro.

File transcription handler supporting multiple providers: Deepgram (default via A/B test), Google Speech-to-Text v1, Chirp 2 (v2). Admin endpoint handleABTestAdmin returns A/B test summary. Uses VertexAIProvider for Google APIs. Supports language detection, sample rate configuration. A/B test routing via transcription-ab service routes between Deepgram and self-hosted Whisper.

Proxy handler for Agent SDK and OpenCode. Routes Claude model requests to Anthropic API, Gemini to Vertex AI. handleVertexModels combines dynamic Anthropic models with hardcoded Gemini models (gemini-3.1-pro, gemini-3-flash). Used for /v1/messages and /anthropic/v1/messages endpoints.

Provider factory (createProvider). Routes models to: AnthropicProvider (Claude), GeminiProvider (Gemini), VertexMaasProvider (GLM-4.7, GLM-5, Kimi K2.5 via GCP), OpenRouterProvider (DeepSeek, Llama, Qwen via OpenRouter), OpenAIProvider (default). Special case: screenpipe-event-classifier routes to self-hosted vLLM.

Usage tracking and rate limiting service. Manages per-device daily query limits per tier (anonymous=25, logged_in=50, subscribed=1500). Tracks IP-based abuse. Integrates credit system via Supabase RPC (deduct_credits). Model weights affect quota consumption (Claude Opus=15, Sonnet=3, Haiku=1, free models=0). Exposes getTierConfig, trackUsage, getUsageStatus, isModelAllowed.

Model health tracking via D1 model_health table. Logs outcomes (ok/error/rate_limited/timeout) per model. Computes rolling 5-minute error rates. Health status: healthy (<30% errors), degraded (30-80%), down (>80%). Prunes records older than 1 hour.

Transcription A/B test routing between Deepgram and self-hosted Whisper. Supports dual-send comparisons. Exposes runTranscriptionABTest, logAllABTestResults, getABTestSummary, getWhisperTrafficPct, getSelfHostedUrl.

CORS utilities: addCorsHeaders (sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *, methods, headers, max-age), handleOptions (CORS preflight), createSuccessResponse (with CORS + JSON/text content-type), createErrorResponse (with CORS + JSON).

Voice utilities: validateAudioInput, transcribeAudio, textToSpeech. Supports Deepgram transcription models (nova-2, nova-3, enhanced, whisper) and Deepgram TTS voices (aura-asteria-en through aura-zeus-en).

Core type definitions for ai-gateway. Env interface defines all environment variables (API keys, D1 DB, rate limiter, Vertex credentials, Supabase, Sentry, etc.). Defines UserTier, AuthResult, TierLimits, UsageResult, UsageStatus, audio types (TranscriptionModelType, TTSVoiceModelType, AudioEncodingType), message/content types for OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini.

data.name: ai-gateway
data.description: Cloudflare Worker AI gateway that proxies to multiple LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Vertex AI, OpenRouter). Handles auth, rate limiting, usage tracking, cost tracking, model health, and transcription services.

Main Cloudflare Worker entry point. Routes all HTTP requests to appropriate handlers based on URL path. Handles OPTIONS preflight, auth validation, rate limiting, and delegates to handlers for each endpoint.

Core type definitions for the ai-gateway. Defines Env (environment bindings), Message, ContentPart, RequestBody, ResponseFormat, UserTier, AuthResult, TierLimits, UsageResult, UsageStatus, and all audio/transcription/TTS types.
Interface implemented by all AI providers. Methods: createCompletion, createStreamingCompletion, formatMessages, formatResponse, listModels. Properties: supportsTools, supportsVision, supportsJson.

Factory function that routes model name to the correct AI provider. Routes: claude/* → AnthropicProvider, gemini/* → GeminiProvider, glm-*/kimi-* → VertexMaasProvider, deepseek/llama/qwen/* → OpenRouterProvider, screenpipe-event-classifier → OpenAIProvider (vLLM), default → OpenAIProvider.

Implements AIProvider for OpenAI API (GPT models). Handles streaming and non-streaming completions, vision (image_url format), JSON response formats, and tool calls. Supports base URL override for custom endpoints.

Implements AIProvider for Anthropic Claude API. Converts OpenAI-format requests to Anthropic Messages API. Features: prompt caching (cache_control on system prompts >= 4096 chars), tool/function calling, vision (base64/image_url), streaming with token usage tracking.

Direct proxy to Anthropic Messages API (bypassing Vertex AI). Normalizes Vertex-style @YYYYMMDD model IDs to Anthropic -YYYYMMDD format. Injects prompt caching on system prompts >= 4096 chars. Returns 503 on spending limits, 429 on rate limits. Used by Agent SDK and OpenCode.
Fetches available Claude models dynamically from Anthropic API. Falls back to hardcoded list: claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001.

Implements AIProvider for Google Gemini API. Features: automatic web search detection (via tool or intent fallback), function calling, vision (inlineData/fileData formats), prompt caching via cache_control. Includes direct web search execution via googleSearch grounding.

Implements AIProvider for Google Cloud Vertex AI (Claude via GCP). Generates RSA-signed JWT for service account auth, caches access tokens. Converts OpenAI-format requests to Anthropic Messages API for Vertex. Has 30-second idle timeout on streams.

Passthrough proxy for Anthropic-format requests to Vertex AI. Sanitizes messages (fixes nested {text: {text: '...'}} bug), maps model IDs to Vertex format (claude-haiku-4-5@20251001). Used by Agent SDK.
Fixes common message formatting issues: unwraps nested text objects, parses tool call arguments from strings to objects, sanitizes content blocks recursively.

Converts model IDs and applies aliases for Vertex AI. Maps: claude-haiku-4-5@20251001, claude-opus-4-5@20251101, claude-sonnet-4-5 → haiku (Sonnet not available on Vertex). Converts date suffix format from -YYYYMMDD to @YYYYMMDD.

Implements AIProvider for Vertex AI MaaS (Managed, pay-per-token). Serves open-source models (GLM-4.7, GLM-5, Kimi K2.5, Llama 4 Maverick/Scout) via GCP managed endpoint. Zero cost to users (burns GCP credits). Reuses VertexAIProvider for auth.

Resolves short model IDs to Vertex MaaS publisher/model IDs. Maps: glm-4.7 → zai-org/glm-4.7-maas, glm-5 → zai-org/glm-5-maas, kimi-k2.5 → moonshotai/kimi-k2-thinking-maas, llama-4-maverick → meta/llama-4-maverick-17b-128e-instruct-maas.

Implements AIProvider for OpenRouter (aggregates DeepSeek, Llama, Qwen, Mistral). Uses screenpipe referrer header. Handles vision (base64/image_url), JSON schemas, and streaming. Models: qwen3.5-flash, deepseek-chat, deepseek-v3.2-speciale, llama-4-scout, llama-4-maverick, qwen3-coder:free, step-3.5-flash:free.

Handles POST /v1/chat/completions. Auto model waterfall: tries glm-5 → kimi-k2.5 → glm-4.7 → qwen3.5-flash → gemini-3-flash (or vision waterfall with llama-4-maverick). Prepends screenpipe system hint. Logs model outcome (ok/error/rate_limited) to model_health table.

Ordered list of free/near-free models for auto-routing: [glm-5, kimi-k2.5, glm-4.7, qwen/qwen3.5-flash-02-23, gemini-3-flash]. Vision waterfall: [llama-4-maverick, gemini-3-flash, llama-4-scout, gemini-2.5-flash].

Handles GET /v1/models. Returns curated model catalog (ModelEntry objects) filtered by user tier. Attaches live health status from model_health D1 table. OpenAI-compatible response format with extra metadata (cost_tier, recommended_for, warning, health).

Hardcoded model catalog with 18 models. Free: auto, glm-4.7, glm-5, kimi-k2.5, llama-4-maverick, llama-4-scout, gemini-3-flash. Paid: claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-5, claude-haiku-4-5, qwen3.5-flash, deepseek-chat, deepseek-v3.2-speciale, qwen3.5-397b, llama-4-scout, llama-4-maverick, gemini-3.1-pro.

Handles POST /v1/listen. Routes to Deepgram (default), Google Speech-to-Text v1 (chirp), or Chirp 2 based on x-transcription-provider header. Runs A/B test between providers. Logs cost at $0.26/hr audio.
Transcribes audio via Deepgram nova-3 model. Uses runTranscriptionABTest which handles A/B routing and dual-send comparison logging.
Transcribes audio via Google Cloud Speech-to-Text v1 with latest_long model. Uses VertexAIProvider for auth. Returns Deepgram-compatible response format.
Transcribes audio via Google Cloud Speech-to-Text v2 with Chirp 2 model. Uses VertexAIProvider for auth. Auto-decoding config, multi-language support.
Handles POST /v1/voice/transcribe. Validates audio input, calls transcribeAudio with Deepgram. Returns transcription, confidence, language, and word-level timestamps.
Handles POST /v1/voice/query. Transcribes audio, then sends to AI model (default gpt-5.4) for text response. Returns transcription + AI response.
Handles POST /v1/text-to-speech. Converts text to audio using Deepgram TTS (aura-asteria-en default voice). Supports 12 aura-* voice models. Returns audio/wav.
Handles POST /v1/voice/chat. Full voice pipeline: transcribe audio → AI model → TTS. Returns audio/wav with X-Transcription and X-AI-Response headers.
Handles POST /v1/web-search. Executes web search via Gemini's Google Search grounding. Returns content and source URLs.
Handles /v1/messages (Agent SDK) and /anthropic/v1/messages (OpenCode). Routes to proxyToAnthropic (direct Anthropic API) since it replaced Vertex proxy.
Handles GET /anthropic/v1/models. Combines dynamic Claude models from Anthropic API with static Gemini models list.

Validates auth from request headers. Supports: no auth → anonymous tier, UUID token → logged_in, Clerk user_id → logged_in, Clerk JWT → subscribes if has subscription, screenpipe JWT → checks cloud_subscribed, invalid token → still anonymous (fail-open). Uses verifyClerkToken, validateScreenpipeToken, validateSubscriptionWithId.
Verifies JWT token from Clerk using CLERK_SECRET_KEY. Returns { valid: boolean, userId?: string }.

Checks Supabase cloud_subscriptions table for active/trialing subscription. Handles UUID (Supabase user ID) and Clerk user_id (user_xxx pattern, resolves to UUID then checks). Returns { isValid, userId }.
Validates screenpipe JWT against screenpi.pe/api/user. Returns { isValid, userId, hasSubscription }.

Checks request against rate limits using Cloudflare Durable Objects (RateLimiter class). Uses device ID or IP as identifier. Tier-specific RPM limits with endpoint multipliers (voice/transcribe at 0.75x, voice/query at 0.5x, voice/chat at 0.4x). Fails open on errors.
Cloudflare Durable Object for per-identifier rate limiting. Tracks request count in 60-second windows. Endpoint-specific RPM multipliers.

Tracks API usage in D1 usage table. Checks daily query limits (weighted by model). Falls back to credit deduction when free quota exhausted. IP-based abuse prevention for anonymous users. Auto-reload check when credits <= 10.
Returns current usage without incrementing. Includes tier, used_today, limit_today, remaining, resets_at, model_access, credits_balance, upgrade_options.
Checks if a model is allowed for a given tier based on tier allowlist. Subscribed users can use any model (*). Others checked against allowedModels list with fuzzy matching.

Returns tier limits (dailyQueries, rpm, allowedModels) with env var overrides (LIMIT_ANONYMOUS_DAILY, LIMIT_SUBSCRIBED_RPM, etc.). Defaults: anonymous=25/day 15rpm, logged_in=50/day 25rpm, subscribed=1500/day 60rpm.

Returns query cost weight for a model (affects daily quota consumption). auto=0, vertex-maas=0, claude-opus=15, claude-sonnet=3, claude-haiku=1, gemini-3-pro=3, deepseek-v3.2-speciale=3, others=1. Free models (weight=0) don't count toward quota.
Resolves userId to clerk_id for credit lookups. user_credits table uses clerk_id (user_xxx) as key. Caches UUID → clerk_id mappings in module-level Map.
Calls Supabase RPC deduct_credits to deduct 1 credit. Used when free daily quota exhausted. Returns { success, remaining }.
Queries user_credits table for current credit balance (requires clerk_id resolution). Used in error responses and status.
Inserts cost record into D1 cost_log table. Fields: device_id, user_id, tier, provider, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, estimated_cost_usd, endpoint, stream.

Calculates estimated cost using per-million pricing. Fuzzy-matches model string to MODEL_PRICING table. When tokens unknown, uses DEFAULT_INPUT_TOKENS=2000 and DEFAULT_OUTPUT_TOKENS=500. Unknown models default to $0.01.
Infers provider from model name: claude→anthropic, gpt/o1/o3→openai, gemini→google, glm-/kimi-→vertex-maas, deepseek/llama/qwen/mistral/step→openrouter, screenpipe-event→screenpipe-vllm.
Sums estimated_cost_usd from cost_log for today for a device. Enforces per-user daily cost caps (default $5/day, subscribed $25/day). Used to gate expensive model usage.
Admin endpoint: aggregates cost data over N days. Returns: total_cost_usd, total_requests, avg_cost_per_request, daily breakdown, by_model, by_provider, by_tier.
Per-million-token pricing table. Vertex MaaS=0, Claude haiku=$0.80/$4.00, sonnet=$3/$15, opus=$15/$75, Qwen3.5-flash=$0.065/$0.26, Deepseek-chat=$0.26/$0.38, Gemini-2.5-flash=$0.15/$0.60.
Logs request outcome (ok/error/rate_limited/timeout) for a model to D1 model_health table. Fire-and-forget.
Queries rolling 5-minute error rates from model_health table. Returns status: healthy (error_rate<30%), degraded (30-80%), down (>=80%). Used by /v1/models to show live model health.
Deletes model_health records older than 1 hour. Called opportunistically from /v1/models endpoint.

Runs transcription A/B/C routing. Picks primary provider based on DEEPGRAM_TRAFFIC_PCT/WHISPER_TRAFFIC_PCT/PARAKEET_TRAFFIC_PCT. If dual-send enabled (DUAL_SEND_PCT), calls ALL providers in parallel for dataset building. Falls back to Deepgram if primary fails.
Random roll to pick transcription provider based on traffic percentages. Returns { primary, dualSend }.
Calls Deepgram API with nova-3 model, smart_format. 2 retry attempts on 502/503/520/522/524/429. 30-second timeout.
Inserts A/B test result into D1 transcription_ab_test table. Records: provider, latency_ms, audio_bytes, estimated_duration_s, transcript_length, status, device_id, comparison_* fields for dual-send.
Returns singleton DeepgramClient instance using DEEPGRAM_API_KEY. Reuses connection across requests.

Wraps a streaming Response to extract token usage from SSE events. Supports 'anthropic' format (message_start/input_tokens, message_delta/output_tokens) and 'openai' format (usage in final chunk). Returns { response, usage: Promise }.
Validates audio request: checks Content-Type is audio/*, parses content type (wav/mp3/flac/ogg/webm), verifies non-empty, enforces 100MB size limit.
Transcribes audio using Deepgram SDK (nova-3 model). Options: language, detectLanguage, smart_format, diarize, punctuate, sample_rate. Returns { text, confidence, language, words }.
Converts text to speech using Deepgram API (aura-asteria-en default voice). Options: voice (12 aura-* models), encoding (linear16/mp3). Returns ArrayBuffer audio.

Validates user subscription via Supabase RPC has_active_cloud_subscription. Caches results for 5 minutes. Handles UUID (Supabase user ID), Clerk user_id (user_xxx), and JWT tokens (screenpipe desktop app).
In-memory cache for subscription status. 5-minute TTL. Keyed by userId (UUID, Clerk user_id, or JWT). Used to reduce Supabase API calls.
Adds CORS headers to Response: Access-Control-Allow-Origin=*, Allow Methods=GET/HEAD/POST/OPTIONS, Allow Headers=*, Credentials=true, Max-Age=86400.
Returns CORS preflight response for OPTIONS requests. Includes Access-Control-Request-Headers if provided.
Creates Response with status 200, CORS headers, Content-Type application/json (or text/plain if body is string).
Creates Response with error JSON body { error: message }, given HTTP status, with CORS headers.

nodes.description: Unified E2E test package for screenpipe — CLI, API, and app UI. Runs locally (Tart VM) or in CI.
Local macOS VM testing CLI. Commands: setup, test, ssh, status, teardown. Orchestrates Tart VM lifecycle and test execution.
Tart VM management library. Handles install, VM lifecycle (create/start/stop/delete), SSH, SCP, and file transfer to macOS VM.
VM test runner that uses shared suites over SSH + port forwarding to a Tart macOS VM. Deploys binary, manages server lifecycle.
Unified E2E test runner for local execution. Runs API and CLI test suites against a running server.
API test suite — single source of truth for REST API e2e tests. Tests /health, /search endpoints with various parameters.
CLI test suite — single source of truth for CLI binary e2e tests. Tests --help, doctor, status commands.
Model loading test suite — verifies transcription engines (whisper-tiny, parakeet, disabled) start correctly.
Test result type with fields: name, passed, error, durationMs. Used across all test suites.
Execute shell command function type: (command, opts?) => Promise<{stdout, exitCode}>. Abstraction enables local and SSH execution.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand screenpipe's purpose: AI memory for your screen with 24/7 recording, local OCR, transcription, and search.

tour.title: Desktop App Entry Point
tour.description: The Tauri main.rs initializes plugins, manages state, sets up logging, permissions, tray, and the embedded HTTP server.

tour.title: HTTP API Server
tour.description: The screenpipe-engine server provides search, frames, audio, video, meetings, pipes, and websocket endpoints via Axum router.

tour.title: Screen Capture Pipeline
tour.description: screenpipe-screen handles monitor enumeration, frame comparison, window capture, and OCR (Apple Vision, Tesseract, Windows OCR).

tour.title: Audio Pipeline
tour.description: screenpipe-audio provides audio capture and transcription with VAD (voice activity detection) and speaker diarization.

tour.title: Database Layer
tour.description: screenpipe-db manages SQLite storage for frames, OCR text, audio transcriptions, UI events, and full-text search with FTS5.

tour.title: AI Agents (Pipes)
tour.description: Pipes are scheduled AI agents (markdown files with YAML frontmatter). screenpipe-engine provides pipe_store and pipes_api for managing pipe execution history.

tour.title: Frontend UI
tour.description: React components built with Tauri API for the desktop UI, including onboarding, pipe management, settings, and search interfaces.
